steps.title: Identify the pattern
steps.content: AACM advances young Black musicians by pairing them with established mentors for technical training and creative guidance. The blank follows: "Numerous organizations offer similar kinds of support to new generations of painters, writers, and other artists, suggesting that ______"

steps.title: Generalize the inference
steps.content: If many other arts organizations do the same mentorship-based thing for young artists, the logical inference is that arts organizations broadly recognize mentorship of young artists as valuable.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'technical training... more than... creative guidance': the passage treats them as joint benefits, not ranks one above the other. Overreach. ✗
  • Choice B — 'many arts organizations recognize the importance of... young artists... learn from experienced mentors': directly generalizes the "similar kinds of support" sentence. ✓
  • Choice C — 'established artists could become even better... by serving as mentors': passage says mentors help mentees, not that mentoring improves the mentors. Misread. ✗
  • Choice D — 'more important for musicians than... painters, writers': opposite of the point — the passage groups them together as similarly mentored. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
